heading: Use of brewery
label: 5411 Use of brewery
text: The brewery shall be used under regulations prescribed by the Secretary only for the purpose of producing, packaging, and storing beer, cereal beverages containing less than one-half of 1 percent of alcohol by volume, vitamins, ice, malt, malt sirup, and other byproducts and of soft drinks; for the purpose of processing spent grain, carbon dioxide, and yeast; and for such other purposes as the Secretary by regulation may find will not jeopardize the revenue.
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 5411, , , consisted of provisions similar to those comprising this section, prior to the general revision of this chapter by .act Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 675Pub. L. 85–859

Amendments1976— struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 94–4551971— struck out requirement of separate facilities for bottling of beer and cereal beverages and permitted use of brewery for packaging and storing beer and other cereal beverages.Pub. L. 91–673

Effective Date of 1971 AmendmentAmendment by effective on first day of first calendar month which begins more than 90 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 91–673section 5 of Pub. L. 91–673section 5056 of this titleJan. 12, 1971
